subject: Re: EPU folder structure with Warp
body:
Hi Andreas

Thanks for your reply. I think we will try something along those lines, 
i.e. using links. In the past we did mount the storage server to a Windows 
PC (The TFS Amira PC which has the GTX 1070 GPU)  but encountered the issue 
of the mounted folder nor refreshing as new movies where arriving. 
Its something we want to revisit though. If we sort something out different 
from you Ill update this post.

Thanks

Christos

On Wednesday, February 12, 2020 at 12:58:50 PM UTC, Andreas wrote:
>
> Hi Christos,
>
> The script we use to move files to the storage server also makes a 
> warp-folder in with symlinks to all the movies from a data collection. We 
> then process using Warp on a dedicated Windows computer with the storage 
> server mounted. Perhaps a similar solution, but making the symlinks 
> directly on the K3 PC, could work for you? I don't know how easy it is to 
> work with symlinks in scripts directly in Windows, though.
>
> /Andreas
>
>
> tirsdag den 11. februar 2020 kl. 15.21.52 UTC+1 skrev Christos Savva:
>>
>> Hi
>>
>> We write EPU K3 Tiff files on the K3 PC SSD array and then copy them to 
>> our storage server for further processing. I would like to use Warp on the 
>> K3 PC and have it read the files that are in the 
>> \DoseFractions\EPUSessionFolder\Images-Disk\GridSquarexxxxx\Data\... which 
>> according to the website isn't possible as all micrographs have to bee in 
>> the same folder. Ideally  I dont want to use stacker to move the files to 
>> another location but can do if there aren't other options. Can I ask what 
>> other EPU users that process files on the K3 PC with Warp are doing?
>>
>> Thanks
>>
>>
>> Christos
>>
>
